name: Tex-Mex Roast Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-size bowl, mix together the lime juice, chicken stock, red wine vinegar, garlic, green onions, chili powder, ground cumin, cumin seeds, cayenne pepper or jalapeno pepper, cilantro, and lime zest.
2. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
3. Place the chicken in a large bowl or resealable plastic bag and cover with the marinade. Marinate for at least 1 hour and up to 24 hours in the refrigerator.
4. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
5. Place the marinated chicken, along with the marinade, in a roasting pan. Add more stock if needed so it comes 1 inch up the side of the chicken.
6. Bake, basting every 20 minutes, for 1 to 1 1/4 hours, or until a meat thermometer registers 180 degrees when inserted into the thigh.
Yield: 4 servings
